differentApproach.sectionAria: How Rise Up Kids differs from traditional English programs
differentApproach.title: A different approach
differentApproach.p1: Traditional programs largely depend on memorization and translation.
differentApproach.p2: Rise Up Kids is built based on how children naturally acquire language: through context, interaction, and meaningful repetition.
differentApproach.p3: Students don't just learn English, they come to understand and use it naturally.
differentApproach.p4: More than studying English, students come to live it through real and playful experiences.

educationSpecialists.sectionAria: What education specialists say about Rise Up Kids
educationSpecialists.title: What Education Specialists Say About the Rise Up Kids Methodology
educationSpecialists.subtitle: Perspectives from education professionals on a more natural and effective approach to English learning.

grant.name: Dr. Grant Eckstein, Ph.D.
grant.role: Professor of Linguistics at Brigham Young University
grant.quote: As a scholar in applied linguistics and coordinator of a TESOL graduate program, I evaluate language learning programs through both research and practice. Rise Up Kids stands out as a program that successfully brings these together. Its design reflects well-established principles of second language acquisition, including meaningful input, careful scaffolding, and opportunities for engagement that are essential for young learners. These principles are thoughtfully embedded in the structure of the lessons. The quality of the materials is equally impressive. The videos and lessons are engaging, well-produced, and clearly designed to sustain children's attention while supporting real language development. What distinguishes Rise Up Kids most is its clear commitment to excellence. The program demonstrates both pedagogical expertise and a deep understanding of how children learn languages. I would confidently recommend it to educators and families seeking high-quality English instruction.

sarah.name: Sarah Schumutz
sarah.credentials: Specializations in Elementary Mathematics and Teaching English to Language Learners at BYU.
sarah.quote: As an elementary educator with experience supporting multilingual learners, I understand how challenging it can be for children to truly acquire English, even when they are surrounded by the language every day. Many immigrant students in the United States still struggle to develop confidence and meaningful communication skills in English despite years of exposure. What impressed me about Rise Up Kids is how intentionally the program is designed to keep children actively engaged in the learning process. The lessons encourage participation, comprehension, movement, and interaction in ways that help language become meaningful and memorable for young learners. The structure behind the program is thoughtful and purposeful, while the videos and activities remain fun and age appropriate. Rise Up Kids creates the kind of learning environment that helps children feel successful, motivated, and genuinely connected to the language.

getStarted.sectionAria: How schools get started with Rise Up Kids
getStarted.title: How Schools Get Started

step1.title: 1. Pilot Group
step1.description: Start with a pilot classroom to experience the approach firsthand.

step2.title: 2. Guided Implementation
step2.description: Initial training with close support throughout the first weeks of implementation.

step3.title: 3. Gradual Expansion
step3.description: Expand the curriculum to additional classrooms after the first results.

getStarted.footer: We work with a limited number of schools to provide close support and maintain quality.

items.question: What age groups is the program designed for?
items.answer: Rise Up Kids is designed for children ages 5 to 10, focusing on comprehension, speaking, and natural language use. Through this approach, students begin to understand and communicate with greater confidence in the classroom.

items.question: When do schools begin to see progress?
items.answer: The first signs are observed through student interactions, including faster responses, better comprehension, and more active class participation. With consistent exposure, students naturally begin to understand and use English with greater confidence.

items.question: Do teachers need to be fluent in English?
items.answer: Teachers don't need to be native speakers, but should have a good level of English, preferably advanced. We provide clearly structured lessons and implementation support, allowing teachers to feel confident while students are consistently exposed to accurate pronunciation through the digital curriculum.

items.question: How does the program fit into the school routine?
items.answer: The program is built around daily 50-minute lessons integrated into the school routine. The lesson structure makes implementation easy for teachers and ensures a consistent experience for students.

items.question: How can parents follow their child's progress?
items.answer: Parents can track their children's progress through the LMS, where they have access to activities and learning development.

items.question: What is the investment?
items.answer: The investment depends on the number of students and the school structure. During the initial conversation, we present a proposal tailored to your institution's needs and goals.

items.question: How does implementation support work?
items.answer: We provide initial training and ongoing support during the first weeks of implementation, so teachers feel confident applying the program and the rollout is seamless.

items.question: At what age can my child start?
items.answer:
Rise Up Kids is ideal for children ages 5 to 11, the stage with the highest openness to language learning.

Children aged 4 can also participate with close parental support, and learners up to 13 years old are also welcome.

items.question: How much time per day is needed?
items.answer:
The most important factor is frequent exposure to English, not long sessions. Your child can choose to study during Q&A sessions.

We recommend 20 to 30 minutes per day, about 5 times per week.

We also guide how to include English in your child's daily routine in a light and natural way.

items.question: My child doesn't know any English. Is it suitable?
items.answer:
Yes. Rise Up Kids was developed especially for beginners.

The child starts with comprehension, with visual support and guided language, and naturally progresses to speaking, respecting their pace.

items.question: How do I track progress?
items.answer: Parents follow the child's progress throughout the journey through completed activities, participation, and pedagogical guidance when applicable.

items.question: Does the curriculum use any materials?
items.answer:
Yes. The program includes digital experiences and simple, playful materials for hands-on activities.

Parents have access to digital materials included in the program, which can be printed when needed, meaning you do not need to pay for materials.

Some additional materials may be purchased by families, always with guidance within the platform.

items.question: What is the guarantee?
items.answer:
You can try the Rise Up Kids digital program for 14 days.

If it doesn't make sense for your family, we refund the amount within that period.

items.question: Can my child use a computer, phone, or TV?
items.answer:
Yes. Rise Up Kids can be accessed via computer, tablet, or phone.

It can also be mirrored on TV, allowing more comfort and freedom of movement during activities.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.introP1After: platform values the privacy and protection of data of participating families and children. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect personal information in the context of the Rise Up Kids educational platform.
privacyPolicy.introP2: By using the platform, you agree to this Policy.
privacyPolicy.s1P1: Rise Up Kids processes personal data in accordance with applicable data protection laws, including Brazil's Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD), the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), and international children's data protection standards such as the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA), when applicable.
privacyPolicy.s1P2: Data processing is limited, proportionate, and adequate to the educational purposes of the program, with special attention to the protection of children's data and to acting under the responsibility of parents or legal guardians.
privacyPolicy.s2P1: Rise Up Kids acts as the controller of personal data processed in the context of the platform. When we use technical providers to operate the service, they act as data processors, following instructions and adequate security standards.
privacyPolicy.s3P1: We may collect information provided by the responsible party, such as:
s3List: Payment information, processed by a secure payment provider
Child's name and age, or age range
Platform usage data, such as progress, completed activities and educational interactions
Content voluntarily submitted by the family, such as speaking activities sent for feedback
privacyPolicy.s3P2: We collect only the data necessary to provide, operate and improve the educational experience.
privacyPolicy.s4P1: Rise Up Kids is an educational service intended for children, used under the responsibility and authorization of parents or legal guardians, in compliance with applicable child protection legislation.
privacyPolicy.s4P2: Children's data is limited to what is necessary for educational personalization, such as name, age or age range, and progress in activities.
privacyPolicy.s4P3: We do not ask children to provide contact information or sensitive data. If improper submission of personal data by children is identified, it may be removed upon request by the responsible party.
privacyPolicy.s5P1: Personal data may be used to:
s5List: Personalize the experience according to age and level
Improve the platform and fix issues
Comply with legal obligations and prevent fraud
privacyPolicy.s6P1: We adopt technical and organizational measures to protect personal data, including:
privacyPolicy.s6P2: No system is entirely immune to risks, but we adopt ongoing practices for adequate protection of information.
privacyPolicy.s7P1: Rise Up Kids does not sell personal data.
privacyPolicy.s7P2: We may share data only when necessary for operation of the service, such as with:
s7List: Essential technical tools for platform operation
privacyPolicy.s7P3: Sharing occurs only to the extent necessary and with adequate protection.
privacyPolicy.s7P4: We may also disclose data when required by law or competent authority.
privacyPolicy.s8P1: We may use cookies and similar technologies to:
privacyPolicy.s8P2: Users can manage cookies in the browser. Some features may not work correctly without essential cookies.
privacyPolicy.s9P1: We retain personal data for as long as necessary to:
s9List: Comply with legal and tax obligations
privacyPolicy.s9P2: After that period, data may be deleted or anonymized, as applicable.
privacyPolicy.s10P1: The legal guardian may request, when applicable:
privacyPolicy.s11P1: Rise Up Kids may use providers and infrastructure located in different countries. When international data transfer occurs, we adopt measures to maintain adequate levels of protection and security, in accordance with applicable legislation.
privacyPolicy.s12P1: We may send communications related to the service, such as:
privacyPolicy.s12P2: The responsible party may opt out of non-essential communications when applicable.
privacyPolicy.s13P1: Rise Up Kids may update this Privacy Policy to reflect improvements, legal changes or evolution of the service. The update date will be revised at the top of the document.
privacyPolicy.s14P1: For questions about privacy or personal data:
